cover.title: The Biggest MOP Wave in a Decade
content: 13,480 HDB flats clear their Minimum Occupation Period in 2026 — nearly double the 6,970 that did in 2025. On paper, this is the largest single-year injection of potential resale supply in recent memory.

But history has a consistent message: no MOP wave in the last decade triggered a genuine price crash. The market absorbed every previous wave. Each time, the predicted collapse failed to materialise. This time, the number is bigger — but so is the nuance.
cover.image: Aerial view of HDB residential estate

slides.title: Timing Is Everything Right Now
content: If you are buying, this is your window. Buyer leverage in Punggol and Tampines improves through the second half of 2026 as MOP sellers enter the market — more choices, more room to negotiate, less pressure to overpay.

If you are a MOP seller, listing early is the edge. Those who get to market in Q1 and Q2, before the bulk of competing inventory arrives, will capture better prices. By Q3 in Punggol, you are competing against dozens of near-identical units in the same precinct.

slides.title: 13,480 HDB Flats Hit MOP in 2026 — Will the Resale Supply Wave Actually Crash Prices?
